contributor_note: Audit draft for existing Windowsill entry WSL-0003. The current production entry uses Ocimum × citriodorum, but multiple taxonomic/gardening references treat lemon basil as Ocimum × africanum, with Ocimum × citriodorum as a synonym or horticultural label. Botanical naming therefore needs human/expert review. Edibility is limited to normal culinary use of leaves and tender stems. Temperature, sun and timing values are modelling estimates based mostly on general basil-growing sources plus lemon-basil-specific secondary sources; expert review is still pending.

habit.note: Fits warm, bright containers and frost-free outdoor contexts. Windowsill fit depends on strong light, warmth and airflow. Pinch flowers early because basil leaf quality and productivity drop after flowering.

notes: Culinary basil with strong lemon/citrus aroma, commonly used in Southeast Asian and other cuisines. Use leaves and tender stems for normal culinary purposes. Treat as cold-sensitive and frost-tender; keep warm, bright and well-drained. Do not treat essential oil, medicinal dosing, extracts, supplements or seed-gel use as covered by this edible-plant entry.
